Notes: Material: blended clip (0.04) + rail (1.02) avg at 70/30 mix = ~0.35 raw; +wastage/scrap buffer. Labor: packaging + inspect at 3 min @ $15/hr. Packaging: poly mailer + tape + optional box. Platform fees: Etsy 6.5% txn + 3% payment = $2.42 total on $24.99 avg; but 1-unit avg order is ~$12. Equipment: $699 P1S at $0.14/hr; amortized per unit at low utilization = high per-unit depreciation. Other: $4.50 blended shipping USPS First Class. Margin depressed by high per-unit overhead at 1/week volume. Break-even on startup capital ~Month 1 at this price point but very thin cash generation.

Material cost improves slightly with 3-spool purchases. Labor per unit improves with light batching discipline (packaging 3–4 orders at once). Platform fees as % revenue stable. Equipment depreciation per unit drops dramatically — printer running 4–5 hr/week at 5/week (vs. 0.5 hr at 1/week). Shipping cost: bundled orders average slightly lower per-unit as AOV rises with bundles. Startup capital recovered in Month 1 at $24.99 avg on ~$650 gross/month.

Target operational throughput — solo, 2–3 hr/day. Material: 10kg bundle rate ($12/kg) reduces cost. Labor: ~90 sec packaging per order, batched shipping. Equipment depreciation negligible per unit — printer running 14+ hr/week. Monthly gross ~$2,500; net profit ~$1,200–$1,400/month after all costs. This is the steady-state sweet spot for solo operation before 2nd printer.

Near-full single printer utilization (35+ hr print/week). Material at 10kg bulk rate. Labor stable — post-processing 1.5 hr/week still manageable solo. Monthly gross ~$6,500; net ~$3,500–$4,000. Begin planning 2nd printer at this level. No contractor needed yet. Packaging moves to case quantities for slight savings.

2nd printer acquired (adds ~$0.05/unit depreciation). Contractor post-processing labor introduced at 10 hrs/week ($15/hr = $600/month → ~$0.12/unit). Labor/unit increases vs. 50/week as contractor rate absorbed. Monthly gross ~$13,000; net ~$7,000–$8,500. 2nd printer payback in 3–5 weeks. Consider FBA onboarding to handle volume without proportional order management time.

Throughput Level: Material basis: PLA+ at $12/kg for 10kg+ bulk orders (eSUN or Overture). Blended unit weight: clips 3–4g; rails 85–100g. Mix assumption: 70% clip orders (3-unit bundles) / 30% rail orders by unit count. Blended material cost per shipped unit accounts for this mix.

Labor basis: $15/hour opportunity cost. Includes harvest + inspect + packaging + shipping label. Does NOT include print monitoring (passive). At 20/week: ~90 sec per order. At 100/week: contractor introduced for 10 hrs/week.
Packaging: poly mailer ($0.08) + zip bags ($0.03) + thank-you card ($0.05) + optional box for rails ($0.25–0.40). Average across mix.

Platform fees: Etsy 6.5% transaction fee + ~3% payment processing on $24.99 average order = $2.37 total fees. Revenue/Unit in this model is the NET after fees column J already subtracts fees — Platform Fees/Unit column shows the fee component of COGS for clarity.

Equipment: Bambu P1S at $699, 5,000-hour depreciation life = $0.14/hr. At 20/week: ~14 hr/week × $0.14 = $1.96/week ÷ 20 units = $0.10/unit. At 100/week with 2nd printer: blended depreciation rises slightly.

Other costs: Blended USPS First Class shipping. Clips (120g bundle): $4.00–$4.50. Rails (200g boxed): $5.00–$5.50. Average ~$4.00–$4.25 across mix. Shipping is partially passed to buyer on Etsy (if charging for shipping). Model assumes $4.00–4.50 absorbed into price or added on top — treat as COGS when pricing for free shipping Etsy listings.
Scrap allowance: 5% for mature production (included in material cost). 8–12% for first 2 weeks of production — add $0.10–$0.20/unit to material cost during initial calibration period.

Revenue basis: $24.99 per order (blended — rail orders at $34.99 and 3-clip bundles at $24.99 average to ~$24.99–$29.99 AOV). Model uses $24.99 as conservative baseline. Actual AOV may be higher with bundle discipline.

Break-even analysis: Startup capital ~$950 (printer + filament + packaging supplies). At 20/week generating ~$1,200–$1,400 net/month, startup capital recovered in Month 1. Business is cash-flow positive from the first order month at any production level above 5/week.

Gross Profit/Unit ($): Single-unit orders at $12–$15 have shipping eating 30–37% of revenue. Bundle discipline is critical.

Break-Even Month: Model basis. Assumes active bundle pricing and rail SKU mix.
PETG premium clips at $5+ premium, homelab kit bundles. Requires validated demand.

Packaging Cost/Unit ($): Primary constraint: order demand must consistently exceed single-printer capacity

Labor Cost/Unit ($): Net-positive immediately if it enables handling 100+ vs. 70 units/week
3–5 months in savings vs. Amazon bundles
Packaging Cost/Unit ($): Only worthwhile at 330+ units/month; storage overhead below this

Labor Cost/Unit ($): Incremental — FBA revenue net of higher fees
Packaging Cost/Unit ($): FBA fees ~28–32% of revenue vs. Etsy ~9.5%; justified by volume and time savings
Typically triggered around Month 8–12 at strong growth trajectory
